subject: GRB 240608A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 03:01:06 UT on 8 Jun 2024, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 240608A (trigger 739508471.435386 / 240608126).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 144.8, Dec = 38.6 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 09h 39m, 38d 36'), with a statistical uncertainty of 1.2 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 16.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240608126/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn240608126.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240608126/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn240608126.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240608126/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn240608126.gif
